input: Fact A: The company was founded in 2015 by John Smith. Fact B: John Smith started the company in 2015.

expected_output.reason: Both facts state the same information with different wording: company founded in 2015 by John Smith.

input: Fact A: Python was created by Guido van Rossum. Fact B: Guido van Rossum is the creator of the Python programming language.

expected_output.reason: Both facts state the identical relationship between Python and its creator, just with active/passive voice reversal.

input: Fact A: The server uptime reached 99.9% in Q2 2024. Fact B: During the second quarter of 2024, server availability was 99.9%.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the same metric: 99.9% server uptime/availability in Q2 2024, using synonymous terms.

input: Fact A: The patient was diagnosed with type 2 diabetes. Fact B: The patient received a diagnosis of diabetes mellitus type 2.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the same medical diagnosis. 'Type 2 diabetes' and 'diabetes mellitus type 2' are the same condition.

input: Fact A: The marketing campaign generated 5000 new leads in March. Fact B: In March, the marketing campaign brought in 5,000 new leads.

expected_output.reason: Both facts convey identical information: the campaign generated 5,000 new leads in March, only differing in number formatting and sentence structure.

input: Fact A: The concert was held at Madison Square Garden on July 4th. Fact B: On July 4th, a concert took place at Madison Square Garden.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the same event at the same venue on the same date, with only passive/active voice variation.

input: Fact A: The chemical formula for water is H2O. Fact B: Water consists of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the identical chemical composition of water: H2O means two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom.

input: Fact A: The first iPhone was released by Apple in 2007. Fact B: Apple launched the original iPhone back in 2007.

expected_output.reason: Both facts state the same event: Apple's release of the first iPhone in 2007, using synonyms 'released' and 'launched'.

input: Fact A: The total revenue for FY2023 was 12.5 million dollars. Fact B: Fiscal year 2023 revenue totaled $12.5 million.

expected_output.reason: Both facts report the identical revenue figure of $12.5 million for fiscal year 2023, with only formatting differences.

input: Fact A: The software update requires at least 8GB of RAM to install. Fact B: To install the software update, a minimum of 8 gigabytes of memory is needed.

expected_output.reason: Both facts state the same system requirement: 8GB of RAM is the minimum for installation, with 'RAM' and 'memory' used interchangeably in this context.

input: Fact A: The merger between the two companies was finalized on December 1, 2023. Fact B: The two companies completed their merger on the first of December, 2023.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the same merger event on the same date, with different date format and synonymous verbs.

input: Fact A: The Earth orbits the Sun at approximately 30 kilometers per second. Fact B: The Earth's orbital velocity around the Sun is roughly 30 km/s.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the same physical quantity: Earth's orbital speed around the Sun at approximately 30 km/s, with different terminology.

input: Fact A: The GDPR regulation went into effect on May 25, 2018. Fact B: GDPR became enforceable starting May 25th, 2018.

expected_output.reason: Both facts state that GDPR became effective/enforceable on the same date, using slightly different legal terminology.

input: Fact A: The system update includes a new dark mode feature. Fact B: A dark mode feature has been added as part of the system update.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the same feature addition: dark mode included in the system update.

input: Fact A: The new policy reduced workplace accidents by 40%. Fact B: There was a 40% decrease in workplace accidents following the implementation of the new policy.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the same outcome: a 40% reduction in workplace accidents attributed to the new policy.

input: Fact A: The experimental drug showed a 72% response rate in phase 2 clinical trials. Fact B: In phase 2 trials, 72% of patients responded to the investigational drug.

expected_output.reason: Both facts report the same clinical trial result: 72% response rate in phase 2, with 'experimental drug' synonymous with 'investigational drug'.

input: Fact A: The company's carbon emissions decreased by 25% from 2020 to 2023. Fact B: Between 2020 and 2023, the company cut its carbon footprint by a quarter.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the same 25% reduction in carbon emissions over the same time period, with '25%' and 'a quarter' being equivalent.

input: Fact A: The cybersecurity breach exposed the personal data of 3.2 million users. Fact B: The personal data of 3.2 million users was compromised by a cybersecurity breach.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the same breach incident affecting 3.2 million users, with 'exposed' and 'compromised' as synonyms.

input: Fact A: The company's shares rose by 12% after the earnings report was released. Fact B: Following the earnings report release, the company's stock price increased by 12 percent.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the same 12% stock price increase triggered by the earnings report.

input: Fact A: The team won the championship with a score of 3-1 in the final match. Fact B: The final match ended 3-1, crowning the team as champions.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the same championship outcome with the same score of 3-1 in the final match.

input: Fact A: The flight from New York to London was delayed by 3 hours due to weather conditions. Fact B: Weather conditions caused the New York to London flight to be delayed by three hours.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the same flight delay with the same cause and duration, differing only in active/passive voice.

input: Fact A: The project was completed under budget at $1.8 million, saving $200,000. Fact B: The project finished $200,000 under the $2 million budget, costing $1.8 million.

expected_output.reason: Both facts convey the same financial outcome: a $2M budget project completed at $1.8M with $200K savings.

input: Fact A: The database migration was completed on March 15, 2024. Fact B: DB migration to PostgreSQL 15 finished on 2024-03-15.

expected_output.reason: Both facts refer to the same database migration event on the same date, with Fact B providing additional detail about the target version.

input: Fact A: The athlete broke the world record with a time of 9.58 seconds. Fact B: The world record was shattered at 9.58 seconds by the athlete.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the identical record-breaking event with the same time, using passive/active voice and 'broke' vs 'shattered'.

input: Fact A: The product launch was delayed by two weeks due to supply chain issues. Fact B: Due to supply chain disruptions, the product launch was pushed back by 14 days.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the same delay: two weeks equals 14 days, caused by supply chain issues.

input: Fact A: The forest fire destroyed approximately 15,000 acres of land. Fact B: Roughly 15,000 acres were burned by the wildfire.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the same fire damage of approximately 15,000 acres, with 'forest fire' and 'wildfire' being synonymous.

input: Fact A: The report stated that the incident happened at 10:00 PM. Fact B: The report stated that the incident happened at approximately 10 PM.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the same incident time. '10:00 PM' and 'approximately 10 PM' refer to the same time, with Fact B using an approximation qualifier.

input: Fact A: The audit found that 15% of transactions had compliance issues. Fact B: The audit found that 15% of the financial transactions reviewed did not comply with internal policies.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the same audit finding of 15% non-compliance in transactions, with Fact B providing more detail about the nature of compliance issues.

input: Fact A: The company reduced its workforce by 10% in 2023 due to economic downturn. Fact B: The company laid off approximately 10% of its employees in 2023.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the same 10% workforce reduction in 2023. Fact A adds the reason, but the core fact is the same.

input: Fact A: The Eiffel Tower is located in Paris, France. Fact B: The Eiffel Tower was constructed in 1889.

expected_output.reason: Fact A describes location, Fact B describes construction date. Different attributes of the same entity.

input: Fact A: The company has 500 employees worldwide. Fact B: The company's annual revenue exceeded $50 million last year.

expected_output.reason: Fact A describes employee count, Fact B describes revenue. Completely different attributes of the same company.

input: Fact A: The patient's blood pressure was 140/90 mmHg during the examination. Fact B: The patient's body temperature was 38.5 degrees Celsius.

expected_output.reason: Fact A reports blood pressure, Fact B reports body temperature. Different vital signs of the same patient.

input: Fact A: The car's top speed is 155 miles per hour. Fact B: The car's fuel efficiency is 32 miles per gallon.

expected_output.reason: Fact A describes top speed, Fact B describes fuel efficiency. Different performance metrics of the same vehicle.

input: Fact A: The university was established in 1865. Fact B: The university has an enrollment of 35,000 students.

expected_output.reason: Fact A describes founding year, Fact B describes current enrollment. Different characteristics of the same institution.

input: Fact A: The earthquake measured 7.2 on the Richter scale. Fact B: The earthquake caused a tsunami that reached the coast 30 minutes later.

expected_output.reason: Fact A describes the magnitude of the earthquake, Fact B describes its consequence. Different aspects of the same event.

input: Fact A: The software supports Windows, macOS, and Linux operating systems. Fact B: The software was first released in January 2020.

expected_output.reason: Fact A describes platform compatibility, Fact B describes release date. Different properties of the same software.

input: Fact A: The actor won an Oscar for Best Actor in 2022. Fact B: The actor starred in the highest-grossing film of 2023.

expected_output.reason: Fact A describes an award won in 2022, Fact B describes a film role in 2023. Different career achievements of the same person.

input: Fact A: The country's GDP grew by 3.5% in the first quarter. Fact B: The country's unemployment rate fell to 4.2% in the same period.

expected_output.reason: Fact A describes GDP growth, Fact B describes unemployment rate. Different economic indicators for the same country.

input: Fact A: The painting was created in 1889. Fact B: The painting depicts a starry night over a village.

expected_output.reason: Fact A describes the creation date, Fact B describes the subject matter. Different attributes of the same artwork.

input: Fact A: The bridge spans 1.7 miles across the bay. Fact B: The bridge was opened to the public in 1937.

expected_output.reason: Fact A describes the length of the bridge, Fact B describes its opening year. Different attributes of the same structure.

input: Fact A: The startup raised $5 million in seed funding from venture capital firms. Fact B: The startup was acquired by a larger competitor for $120 million.

expected_output.reason: Fact A describes the seed funding round, Fact B describes an acquisition. Different events in the company's lifecycle.

input: Fact A: The professor published a groundbreaking paper on quantum computing in 2021. Fact B: The professor teaches advanced algorithms at the university's computer science department.

expected_output.reason: Fact A describes a research publication, Fact B describes a teaching role. Different professional activities of the same person.

input: Fact A: The COVID-19 vaccine developed by Pfizer showed 95% efficacy in clinical trials. Fact B: The Pfizer vaccine received emergency use authorization from the FDA in December 2020.

expected_output.reason: Fact A describes clinical trial efficacy, Fact B describes regulatory authorization. Different milestones for the same vaccine.

input: Fact A: The course covers machine learning fundamentals including supervised and unsupervised learning. Fact B: The course covers machine learning topics such as neural networks, decision trees, and SVMs.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the same course on machine learning, but Fact A mentions broad categories (supervised/unsupervised), while Fact B lists specific algorithms. The topics overlap partially but are not identical.

input: Fact A: The project requires expertise in Python, Django, and PostgreSQL. Fact B: The project requires Python and PostgreSQL skills, plus experience with web frameworks like Django or Flask.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe skill requirements for the same project, but Fact B is more flexible (Django or Flask) while Fact A is specific (Django only). Partial overlap in requirements.

input: Fact A: The conference will feature keynote speakers from Google, Microsoft, and Amazon. Fact B: The conference will have keynote presentations from Google and Microsoft executives.

expected_output.reason: Fact B is a subset of Fact A: Fact A mentions three companies (Google, Microsoft, Amazon), while Fact B only mentions two. The facts partially overlap but are not identical.

input: Fact A: The patient received treatment with antibiotics and was discharged after 3 days. Fact B: The patient was treated with intravenous antibiotics and oral steroids for 5 days.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the patient's treatment, but Fact B adds steroids and specifies IV administration, and the treatment duration differs (3 days vs 5 days). Partial overlap only.

input: Fact A: The company's cloud infrastructure cost increased by 30% in the last quarter. Fact B: The company's overall IT spending grew by 30% in Q3, primarily driven by cloud migration.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe a 30% increase, but Fact A is specifically about cloud infrastructure cost, while Fact B is about overall IT spending with cloud as a driver. The scope differs significantly.

input: Fact A: The system supports both REST and GraphQL APIs for data access. Fact B: The system provides REST API endpoints for data retrieval and manipulation.

expected_output.reason: Fact A mentions both REST and GraphQL, while Fact B only mentions REST. Fact B is a subset of Fact A, omitting the GraphQL support.

input: Fact A: The forest fire destroyed 50,000 acres of land and 200 homes. Fact B: The forest fire burned 50,000 acres of woodland.

expected_output.reason: Fact B is a subset of Fact A: both mention 50,000 acres burned, but Fact A additionally mentions 200 homes destroyed. Incomplete overlap.

input: Fact A: The new feature supports users in the US, UK, and Canada. Fact B: The new feature supports users in the United States, the United Kingdom, Canada, and Australia.

expected_output.reason: Fact B includes Australia in addition to the countries in Fact A. Fact A is a subset of Fact B, so they are not duplicates.

input: Fact A: The server experienced a critical failure at 03:45 UTC and was offline for 2 hours. Fact B: The server ran out of disk space at 03:45 UTC, causing the application to crash.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the same incident at 03:45 UTC, but Fact A attributes the failure to a generic critical failure with 2-hour downtime, while Fact B specifies disk space as the root cause of the crash. The cause differs, making them distinct facts about the same incident.

input: Fact A: The company employs 200 software engineers in its engineering department. Fact B: The company has 200 employees working on software development.

expected_output.reason: Fact A specifies 200 software engineers in the engineering department, while Fact B says 200 employees working on software development. 'Software engineers' is a subset of 'employees working on software development,' which could include QA, PMs, and designers.

input: Fact A: The patient was diagnosed with stage 2 breast cancer. Fact B: The patient was diagnosed with stage 3 breast cancer.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the same type of cancer in the same patient, but the stage differs (2 vs 3). These are contradictory - the patient cannot simultaneously have both stages.

input: Fact A: The company reported a profit of $5 million in Q3. Fact B: The company reported a loss of $5 million in Q3.

expected_output.reason: Fact A states a profit of $5M, Fact B states a loss of $5M. These are directly contradictory financial results for the same quarter.

input: Fact A: The defendant was found guilty by the jury. Fact B: The defendant was acquitted of all charges.

expected_output.reason: Fact A states a guilty verdict, Fact B states an acquittal. These are mutually exclusive legal outcomes.

input: Fact A: The flight departed from Gate A12 at 3:45 PM. Fact B: The flight departed from Gate B7 at 3:45 PM.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the same flight departure time but different gates (A12 vs B7). These are contradictory - the flight cannot depart from two different gates simultaneously.

input: Fact A: The software bug was introduced in version 2.4.1. Fact B: The software bug was introduced in version 2.3.7.

expected_output.reason: Both facts discuss the same bug's origin but specify different versions. These are contradictory statements about when the bug was introduced.

input: Fact A: The experiment showed that the new drug reduces cholesterol by 15%. Fact B: The experiment showed that the new drug has no significant effect on cholesterol levels.

expected_output.reason: Fact A claims a 15% cholesterol reduction, while Fact B claims no significant effect. These are contradictory conclusions from the same experiment.

input: Fact A: The insurance policy covers natural disasters including earthquakes and floods. Fact B: The insurance policy covers natural disasters but excludes flood damage.

expected_output.reason: Fact A says floods are covered, while Fact B explicitly excludes flood damage. These are contradictory statements about the same policy's coverage.

input: Fact A: The product was launched in the US market first, then expanded to Europe six months later. Fact B: The product was launched simultaneously in the US and European markets.

expected_output.reason: Fact A describes a staggered launch (US first, Europe later), while Fact B describes a simultaneous launch. These are contradictory launch strategies.

input: Fact A: The investigation concluded that the fire was caused by an electrical fault. Fact B: The investigation concluded that the fire was caused by arson.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the same fire investigation's conclusion, but the causes differ: electrical fault vs arson. These are mutually exclusive causes.

input: Fact A: The company plans to hire 50 new employees across all departments. Fact B: The company plans to hire 50 new software engineers in the engineering department.

expected_output.reason: Fact A describes 50 hires across all departments, while Fact B specifies 50 software engineers in one department. Fact B is a more specific claim that is not equivalent to Fact A.

input: Fact A: The avian flu outbreak has affected poultry farms in 12 states. Fact B: The avian flu outbreak has affected poultry farms in 12 counties in the Midwest.

expected_output.reason: Fact A says 12 states are affected, Fact B says 12 counties in the Midwest. A county is a subset of a state, and 12 counties is much more specific and geographically narrower than 12 states.

input: Fact A: The study found that regular exercise reduces the risk of heart disease. Fact B: The study found that 30 minutes of daily running reduces the risk of heart disease by 20%.

expected_output.reason: Fact A is a general statement about exercise reducing heart disease risk, while Fact B specifies a particular type of exercise (running), duration (30 min daily), and quantifies the reduction (20%). Fact B is much more specific.

input: Fact A: The company experienced a security incident in Q2. Fact B: The company experienced a ransomware attack that encrypted 500 servers in May.

expected_output.reason: Fact A is a general statement about a security incident in Q2, while Fact B specifies the type (ransomware), impact (500 servers), and month (May). Fact B is a more specific instance of Fact A.

input: Fact A: The athlete won a medal at the Olympics. Fact B: The athlete won a gold medal in swimming at the 2020 Tokyo Olympics.

expected_output.reason: Fact A is a general statement about winning a medal at the Olympics, while Fact B specifies the medal type, sport, and specific Olympic Games. Fact B is a more specific instance.

input: Fact A: The new regulation requires companies to report their carbon emissions. Fact B: The new regulation requires companies with over 500 employees to report their Scope 1, Scope 2, and Scope 3 carbon emissions annually.

expected_output.reason: Fact A is a general statement about reporting requirements, while Fact B specifies the company size threshold, emission scope categories, and reporting frequency. Fact B is a significantly more detailed version.

input: Fact A: The disease affects the respiratory system. Fact B: The disease primarily affects the alveoli in the lungs, causing reduced oxygen exchange.

expected_output.reason: Fact A broadly mentions the respiratory system, while Fact B specifies the exact anatomical structure (alveoli) and mechanism (reduced oxygen exchange). Much more granular.

input: Fact A: The company has a presence in Europe. Fact B: The company has offices in London, Berlin, Paris, and Amsterdam.

expected_output.reason: Fact A is a general statement about European presence, while Fact B lists specific cities with offices. Fact B provides more granular geographic detail.

input: Fact A: The software update improved system performance. Fact B: The software update improved database query response time by 45% and reduced memory usage by 20%.

expected_output.reason: Fact A is a general statement about performance improvement, while Fact B specifies two concrete metrics with percentages. Different levels of granularity.

input: Fact A: The company's product was recalled due to a safety issue. Fact B: The company's model X-200 air fryer was recalled due to a fire hazard caused by faulty wiring in the heating element.

expected_output.reason: Fact A is a general recall statement, while Fact B specifies the exact product model, hazard type, and root cause. Significantly different granularity levels.

input: Fact A: The country implemented economic reforms in 1991. Fact B: The country implemented a series of market-oriented economic reforms in 1991, including trade liberalization, privatization of state-owned enterprises, and foreign investment deregulation.

expected_output.reason: Fact B provides extensive detail about the specific reforms, while Fact A only states that reforms occurred. Fact B is a much more granular description.

input: Fact A: The research paper was published in a scientific journal. Fact B: The research paper was published in Nature, volume 612, issue 7940, pages 345-352, on December 15, 2022.

expected_output.reason: Fact A is a general statement about publication, while Fact B provides the specific journal, volume, issue, pages, and date. Much more granular.

input: Fact A: Artificial Intelligence has transformed many industries in recent years. Fact B: 人工智能近年来已经改变了许多行业。

expected_output.reason: Both facts state the same information in different languages: Fact A is in English, Fact B is the Chinese translation of the same statement.

input: Fact A: The World Health Organization declared COVID-19 a pandemic on March 11, 2020. Fact B: 世界卫生组织于2020年3月11日宣布COVID-19为大流行病。

expected_output.reason: Both facts report the identical event in different languages: WHO declaring COVID-19 a pandemic on March 11, 2020.

input: Fact A: The speed of light in a vacuum is approximately 299,792,458 meters per second. Fact B: 真空中的光速约为每秒299,792,458米。

expected_output.reason: Both facts state the identical physical constant in different languages: the speed of light in a vacuum.

input: Fact A: The human body contains approximately 206 bones. Fact B: 人体大约有206块骨头。

expected_output.reason: Both facts state the identical anatomical fact about the number of bones in the human body, in different languages.

input: Fact A: The Great Wall of China is over 13,000 miles long. Fact B: 中国长城的总长度超过13,000英里。

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the identical measurement of the Great Wall's length in different languages.

input: Fact A: The company achieved ISO 27001 certification in 2023. Fact B: The company achieved International Organization for Standardization 27001 certification in 2023.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the same certification. ISO is the abbreviation for International Organization for Standardization; the full form and abbreviation are equivalent.

input: Fact A: The system uses SSL/TLS encryption for data transmission. Fact B: The system uses Secure Sockets Layer / Transport Layer Security encryption for data transmission.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the same encryption protocol. SSL/TLS is the abbreviation for Secure Sockets Layer / Transport Layer Security.

input: Fact A: The patient's MRI showed no abnormalities in the brain. Fact B: The patient's magnetic resonance imaging scan showed no abnormalities in the brain.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the same medical imaging result. MRI is the standard abbreviation for magnetic resonance imaging.

input: Fact A: The drug is a nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug used to reduce pain and inflammation. Fact B: The drug is an NSAID used to reduce pain and inflammation.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the same drug class. NSAID is the standard abbreviation for nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug.

input: Fact A: The patient has hypertension and requires daily medication. Fact B: The patient has high blood pressure and needs to take medicine every day.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the same medical condition and treatment. 'Hypertension' is the technical term for 'high blood pressure,' and 'requires daily medication' is equivalent to 'needs to take medicine every day.'

input: Fact A: The application uses a NoSQL database for storing unstructured data. Fact B: The application uses a non-relational database for storing unstructured data.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe the same database type. 'NoSQL' and 'non-relational database' are used interchangeably in the tech industry to refer to the same category of databases.

input: Fact A: The company reported a 15% increase in revenue. Fact B: The company reported a 15% increase in profits.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe a 15% increase, but revenue and profit are fundamentally different financial metrics. Revenue is top-line income; profit is bottom-line after expenses.

input: Fact A: The bank approved the loan application at 5.5% interest. Fact B: The bank approved the mortgage application at 5.5% interest.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe a loan approval at 5.5% interest, but 'loan' is a broader category than 'mortgage.' A mortgage is a specific type of loan, so these may or may not refer to the same application.

input: Fact A: The company terminated 15% of its workforce in January. Fact B: The company laid off 15% of its staff in the first quarter.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe a 15% workforce reduction, but Fact A specifies January while Fact B says first quarter. January is within Q1, so Fact B could be the same event with a broader timeframe, or a different round of layoffs.

input: Fact A: The software was tested on over 10,000 devices. Fact B: The software was tested on 10,000 specific device models.

expected_output.reason: Fact A says 'over 10,000 devices' (individual device instances), while Fact B says '10,000 specific device models' (distinct model types). The distinction between device instances and device models is significant.

input: Fact A: The new drug was approved by the FDA for treating diabetes. Fact B: The new drug was approved by the FDA for treating type 2 diabetes in adults.

expected_output.reason: Fact A says the drug is approved for diabetes (which could include type 1 and type 2), while Fact B specifies it is approved only for type 2 diabetes in adults. The scope differs significantly.

input: Fact A: The deposit was made on March 31, 2024. Fact B: The deposit was made on April 1, 2024.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe a deposit but on different dates that are one day apart. These could be the same transaction processed on different dates due to cutoff times, or different transactions.

input: Fact A: The company's stock price dropped by 8% after the CEO resigned. Fact B: The company's stock price dropped by 8% after the CEO announced retirement.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe an 8% stock drop after the CEO's departure, but 'resigned' and 'announced retirement' are different types of departure. Resignation could be abrupt or pressured, while retirement announcement is typically planned.

input: Fact A: The contract requires payment within 30 days of invoice. Fact B: The contract requires payment within 30 business days of invoice.

expected_output.reason: Both facts describe payment terms, but 30 calendar days and 30 business days are significantly different time periods (approximately 42 calendar days). The distinction is subtle but critical.

input: Fact A: The forest fire destroyed 50,000 acres of land. Fact B: The forest fire destroyed 50,000 hectares of land.

expected_output.reason: 50,000 acres and 50,000 hectares are vastly different areas (1 hectare = 2.47 acres). The same numerical value with different units makes these contradictory.

input: Fact A: The API rate limit is set to 1000 requests per hour per API key. Fact B: Each API key is allowed a maximum of 1000 requests per day.

expected_output.reason: Fact A specifies per hour rate limiting while Fact B specifies per day. The time windows differ significantly, making these distinct facts about rate limiting policies.
